top_signals.title: Apple’s $600 Billion Rally Fueled by Traders Fleeing AI Selloff
top_signals.summary: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Indonesia Avoids S&P Rating Outlook Cut in Win for Prabowo
top_signals.summary: Indonesia retained its investment-grade rating and stable outlook at S&P Global Ratings, a reprieve for President Prabowo Subianto and his fiscal policy credibility after a bond market selloff in June.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Rule of law looks shakier 10 years after South China Sea ruling
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: New U-Boot flaws could enable stealthy firmware attacks
top_signals.summary: Six vulnerabilities in the widely used U-Boot bootloader have been discovered that could allow attackers to execute malicious code during device boot, potentially enabling stealthy firmware attacks that compromise security protections and install persistent malware. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Germany’s Smag Sinks in Debut as Defense Stock Appetite Ebbs
top_signals.summary: Smag Mobile Antenna Masts AG shares tumbled as much as 33% below the initial public offering price in their German trading debut, marking the latest sign of waning demand for defense stocks in Europe.

top_signals.title: Traders Grapple With World That’s Good for Dollar, Bad for Bonds
top_signals.summary: What’s good for the US dollar isn’t always good for US bonds — but investors are finding ways to work around it.
